tests.description: Clean inconsistent contact data: normalize names to Title Case, standardize phone format, lowercase emails. Tests real data cleaning ability.

steps.prompt: Clean up the Name column: trim whitespace, fix inconsistent casing to proper Title Case (e.g. 'JOHN SMITH' -> 'John Smith', 'jane doe' -> 'Jane Doe'). Only modify column A. Use set_values only.
Now lowercase all email addresses in column C. Only modify column C. Use set_values only.

tests.description: Add computed columns using formulas: annual total, best quarter, growth rate. Tests formula intelligence beyond basic SUM.

steps.prompt: Add three new columns: F1='Annual Total' with SUM of Q1-Q4 for each product, G1='Best Quarter' showing the MAX quarterly value, H1='Growth %' showing the percentage change from Q1 to Q4 as a formula like =(E2-B2)/B2. Use set_values for headers and set_formula for the computed cells. Write headers in row 1 and formulas in rows 2-5.

tests.description: Vague prompt on messy data with duplicates, blanks, typos. Tests whether LLM can identify and fix real issues without being told exactly what to fix.

steps.prompt: Look at this data and tell me what problems you see.
Fix the status column: normalize all values to lowercase ('active', 'inactive', 'pending'). Fix the typo 'actve' to 'active'. Only modify column C. Use set_values only.

tests.description: Analyst-style questions about financial data. Tests reasoning without writes, then targeted formula addition.

steps.prompt: Analyze this revenue data. Which month had the worst performance and why might that be? What is the overall trend?

Add a Profit column in E (E1='Profit') with formulas E2:E13 computing Revenue minus Expenses for each month. Then add a summary row: A14='Summary', B14=SUM of all revenue, C14=SUM of all expenses, E14=SUM of all profit. Use set_values for labels and set_formula for computed cells.

tests.description: 4-turn conversation building a complete inventory tracker from scratch. Tests context memory and incremental building.

steps.prompt: Create an inventory table with headers: Item, SKU, Quantity, Unit Price, Total Value. Place headers in A1:E1. Add 4 sample items (use realistic office supply items). Use set_values only for everything including the Total Value column (compute the values yourself, don't use formulas).

Add a Reorder Level column in F with header 'Reorder Level'. Set reorder levels based on the items: items with quantity below 20 should have reorder level of 50, others should have reorder level of 25. Use set_values only.
Add 3 more items in rows 6-8 following the same pattern. Include the Reorder Level column too. Make sure the SKU format matches the existing pattern. Use set_values only. Do not modify existing rows.

Now add a summary section below the data. In A10 put 'Total Items:', B10 should have a COUNT formula for column A data rows. In A11 put 'Total Value:', B11 should have a SUM formula for column E data rows. In A12 put 'Avg Price:', B12 should have an AVERAGE formula for column D data rows. Use set_values for labels and set_formula for the computed cells.

tests.description: Existing budget with formulas. Add new rows WITHOUT breaking existing formula references or overwriting the totals row.

steps.prompt: Add two new budget categories: 'Entertainment' with budgeted=150 and actual=200, and 'Savings' with budgeted=500 and actual=500. Insert them as rows 7-8, pushing the current Totals row down to row 9. Update the Totals formulas in row 9 to include the new rows. Use insert_rows, set_values for the new data, and set_formula for the updated totals.

tests.description: Sparse data scattered across the sheet with orphan notes. Tests whether LLM can work with non-contiguous data without touching unrelated cells.

steps.prompt: Observe the data in this sheet. Describe what you see including any isolated values.

Create a proper summary table in A1:C4 with headers Region, Value, Pct of Total. Fill in the three regions (North=100, South=200, East=150) with their values from column C, and calculate each region's percentage of the total (450) as a plain number rounded to 1 decimal. Use set_values only. Do NOT modify F30.

tests.description: Cross-sheet data aggregation: fill department summary from employee data. Tests ability to read one sheet and write computed results to another.

steps.prompt: Fill in the Departments summary table using data from the Employees sheet. For each department: B column = number of employees in that department, C column = average salary (rounded to nearest whole number), D column = total salary budget. Compute the values yourself from the Employees sheet data and use set_values only. Engineering has 3 employees (Alice 95k, Carol 98k, Eve 102k). Marketing has 2 (Bob 72k, Frank 75k). Sales has 2 (Dave 68k, Grace 71k).
